pageInfo.pagePurpose: To create a new, automated, recurring invoice for a customer by defining a schedule, adding line items, and specifying payment terms.

clickableElements.label: Navigate To (Opt+0)
clickableElements.businessIntent: Access a quick navigation menu or command palette.
clickableElements.expectedOutcome: A search bar or menu appears, allowing the user to quickly jump to different parts of the application.

clickableElements.businessIntent: Expand the 'Items' section in the main navigation menu.

clickableElements.businessIntent: Configure a payment gateway to accept online payments.

clickableElements.label: Save
clickableElements.businessIntent: Save the new recurring invoice with the entered details.
clickableElements.expectedOutcome: The form data is submitted, the recurring invoice is created, and the user is likely redirected to a list of recurring invoices.

clickableElements.label: Cancel
clickableElements.businessIntent: Discard the new recurring invoice and exit the creation form.
clickableElements.expectedOutcome: The form is cleared, and the user is returned to the previous page, likely the recurring invoices list.

tables.purpose: To list the products or services that will be included on the recurring invoice, along with their quantities, rates, and total amounts.

columns.name: Drag item up or down
columns.meaning: A handle to reorder the line items on the invoice.
columns.businessPurpose: Allows the user to control the presentation order of items on the final invoice document.

columns.name: Item Details
columns.meaning: The name and description of the product or service being billed.
columns.businessPurpose: Identifies the specific good or service for which the customer is being charged.

columns.name: Quantity
columns.meaning: The number of units of the item being billed.
columns.businessPurpose: Specifies the volume of the product or service sold, used to calculate the total amount.

columns.name: Rate
columns.meaning: The price per unit of the item.
columns.businessPurpose: Defines the unit cost, used in conjunction with quantity to calculate the line item total.

columns.name: Amount
columns.meaning: The total calculated charge for the line item (Quantity x Rate).
columns.businessPurpose: Shows the subtotal for each line item before taxes and discounts.

forms.purpose: To capture all the necessary information to create a new recurring invoice profile, including customer details, schedule, line items, and notes.

fields.businessMeaning: The customer who will receive the recurring invoice.
An internal name or identifier for this recurring invoice schedule.
A reference number, such as a customer's Purchase Order number.
The frequency at which the invoice should be generated (e.g., weekly, monthly).
The date on which the first invoice of this recurring series should be generated.
The date after which no more invoices should be generated for this schedule. Can be left blank if it never expires.
A checkbox likely labeled 'Never Expires' to disable the 'Ends On' date field.
The conditions under which payment is due (e.g., Due on Receipt, Net 30).
The salesperson associated with this sale or customer.
A brief description or subject line that will appear on the invoice.
A field to select a specific price list to apply to the items on this invoice.
Notes or comments that will be visible to the customer on the invoice.
A preference to save these notes for all future recurring invoices for all customers.
The overall discount to be applied to the invoice subtotal, likely as a percentage or fixed amount.
The full terms and conditions of the sale that will be displayed on the invoice.
A preference to save these terms for all future recurring invoices for all customers.

workflows.name: Create New Recurring Invoice
steps: User selects a customer and fills in the profile details like name and order number.
User defines the recurrence schedule including frequency, start date, and end date.
User adds one or more line items to the item table, specifying the item, quantity, and rate.
User customizes the invoice with a subject, customer notes, and terms & conditions.
User applies an overall discount if applicable.
User clicks the 'Save' button to create the recurring invoice profile.

workflows.name: Cancel Recurring Invoice Creation
steps: User starts creating a new recurring invoice.
User decides to discard the changes and clicks the 'Cancel' button.

userGoals.goal: Automate routine billing for a client to save time and ensure consistent invoicing.
evidence: The page is titled 'New Recurring Invoice' and includes fields like 'Repeat Every', 'Start On', and 'Ends On' to define a schedule.

userGoals.goal: Create a detailed template for future invoices to a specific customer.
evidence: The form allows the user to specify line items, 'Subject', 'Customer Notes', and 'Terms & Conditions', which constitute a complete invoice template.

userGoals.goal: Ensure accurate and standardized pricing for recurring services.
evidence: The user can add specific items from a list and select a 'Price List', which helps maintain pricing consistency across invoices.

predictedMissingUI.reason: The label 'Customer Name' exists, but there is no standard input field. The HTML shows a custom element, suggesting a dropdown or modal is used to search for and select an existing customer.
After clicking 'Save', the user needs feedback that the recurring invoice was created successfully. A toast or banner message is a standard UI pattern for this.
Clicking 'Cancel' on a form with data typically triggers a confirmation dialog (e.g., 'Are you sure you want to discard your changes?') to prevent accidental data loss.

pageNarrative: This page is a form for creating a new recurring invoice. Users can select a customer, define a profile name, and set a recurrence schedule with start and end dates. They can add multiple line items to an item table, specifying quantity and rate for each. The page also allows for customization through customer notes, terms and conditions, and an overall discount. Upon completion, the user can save the recurring invoice profile to automate future billing or cancel the process to discard the entry.
